title: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
description: A pilot stranded in the desert meets a young prince fallen to Earth from a tiny asteroid. Through the prince's travels across various planets, the story explores themes of loneliness, friendship, love, and loss. One of the most translated books in history.

author: George Orwell
description: In a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, Winston Smith struggles to maintain his humanity and sanity in a world where independent thinking is a crime. A chilling portrayal of surveillance, propaganda, and the erosion of truth.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Through the eyes of Scout Finch, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel explores racial injustice and the loss of innocence in the American South. Her father, Atticus Finch, defends a Black man falsely accused of a crime, teaching his children powerful lessons about empathy and morality.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins, a comfortable hobbit, is swept into an epic quest to reclaim the lost Dwarf Kingdom of Erebor from the fearsome dragon Smaug. Along the way, he encounters trolls, elves, goblins, and a mysterious ring that holds a dark power.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: Holden Caulfield, a disillusioned teenager, narrates his experiences in New York City after being expelled from prep school. His candid and often alienated perspective on adulthood and society has made this novel a landmark of 20th-century literature.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of manners, morality, and marriage in early 19th-century England. Her evolving relationship with the seemingly proud Mr. Darcy is one of literature's most celebrated love stories, sparkling with wit and social commentary.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: Set in the Jazz Age, this masterpiece follows the mysterious Jay Gatsby and his obsessive love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan. A powerful critique of the American Dream, wealth, and social class in the Roaring Twenties.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: On the desert planet Arrakis, young Paul Atreides finds himself at the center of a galactic struggle for control of the universe's most valuable substance: spice. A sweeping epic of politics, religion, and ecology.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: In a futuristic World State, humans are genetically modified and socially conditioned to serve a ruling order. Happiness is mandatory and dissent is not tolerated. A terrifying vision of a society that has sacrificed freedom for comfort.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Stephen Hawking guides readers through the origins of the universe, the nature of time, black holes, and the search for a unified theory of everything. A landmark of popular science that makes complex concepts accessible to all.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: From the Stone Age to the Silicon Age, Harari explores how Homo sapiens came to dominate the world. A sweeping narrative that examines biology, economics, and culture to understand what it means to be human.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman reveals the two systems that drive human thought: the fast, intuitive System 1 and the slow, deliberative System 2. A groundbreaking exploration of cognitive biases and decision-making.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: Written over 2,500 years ago, this ancient Chinese military treatise remains one of the most influential strategy guides ever written. Its wisdom on leadership, strategy, and human nature transcends warfare to inform modern business and life.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: Tara Westover recounts her extraordinary journey from growing up in a strict, survivalist family in rural Idaho to earning a PhD from Cambridge University. A powerful memoir about the value of education and the pain of family separation.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy, embarks on a journey to Egypt after dreaming of a treasure. His quest teaches him to listen to his heart and recognize omens. A fable about following one's dreams and finding one's destiny.

title: The Name of the Rose
author: Umberto Eco
description: In 1327, Brother William of Baskerville arrives at a wealthy Italian abbey to investigate a series of mysterious deaths. Combining medieval theology, semiotics, and detective work, this novel is both a gripping mystery and a profound intellectual puzzle.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: In a future society where books are banned, fireman Guy Montag burns any that are found. But as he begins to question his role and the emptiness of his life, he makes a dangerous choice that changes everything.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: In a post-apocalyptic world, a father and son journey across a devastated American landscape toward the coast, facing starvation, brutality, and the complete breakdown of civilization. A haunting story of survival and unconditional love.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In the Republic of Gilead, a totalitarian state, women are reduced to their reproductive functions. Offred, a Handmaid, navigates this brutal world while clinging to memories of her former life. A powerful feminist dystopia.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
description: Jared Diamond investigates why certain civilizations conquered others, arguing that geography and environment — not racial superiority — shaped the modern world. A Pulitzer Prize-winning exploration of history, biology, and sociology.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: James Clear presents a proven framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones — one tiny change at a time. Packed with scientific research and practical strategies, this book is a roadmap to personal transformation.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
description: Charles Duhigg explores the science behind why habits exist and how they can be changed. Through real-life stories — from Olympic athletes to corporate boardrooms — this book reveals how understanding habits can transform lives and organizations.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor E. Frankl
description: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl recounts his experiences in Nazi concentration camps and introduces logotherapy — a therapeutic approach centered on finding purpose in all forms of existence. A profound testament to human resilience.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: The private thoughts of Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius, written as personal reflections on Stoic philosophy. Covering virtue, reason, and mortality, this timeless work offers wisdom on living with clarity and inner calm amidst chaos.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
description: Drawing from 3,000 years of history, Robert Greene distills the principles of power into 48 essential laws. From Machiavelli to Sun Tzu, this controversial and influential book reveals the timeless strategies of the world's most powerful figures.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
description: Mark Manson flips conventional self-help wisdom on its head, arguing that the key to a good life isn't avoiding problems but learning which ones are worth caring about. A raw, irreverent, and surprisingly profound guide to living better.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
description: Anne Frank's diary, written while hiding from the Nazis in Amsterdam during World War II, is a moving testament to the human spirit. Her honest and intimate account of fear, hope, and adolescence remains deeply affecting.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Raskolnikov, a destitute ex-student in St. Petersburg, commits a murder he believes is justified by his superior intellect. What follows is a devastating psychological journey through guilt, morality, and the search for redemption.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: The multi-generational saga of the Buendía family in the mythical town of Macondo. A masterpiece of magical realism blending the extraordinary with the mundane, love with loss, and history with myth.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: Dorian Gray, a young man of extraordinary beauty, wishes that his portrait would age instead of him. As he indulges in a life of hedonism and vice, the painting becomes a monstrous reflection of his soul while he remains outwardly unchanged.
